technical_subject.diagram_prompt: Technical exploded schematic: four-way reversing valve body, slide piston, capillary tubes to indoor and outdoor coils, solenoid pilot; cyan schematic lines on navy field, part callouts, accurate topology not decorative

config.voice_suffix: deep measured baritone, unhurried authoritative systems walk-through, synthetic presenter only

shots.video_prompt: CONTINUITY LOCK @Elijah-001: identical presenter face, workwear wardrobe, blueprint bench set, same eye-line to camera — seamless continuation of prior take, zero jump cut. CONTINUITY LOCK @Set-Warehouse-Industrial-001: identical industrial loft — same window grid camera-left, same concrete floor, same brick tone, same beam geometry — seamless continuation, zero jump cut. STYLE LOCK @Style-Blueprint-Technical-001: blueprint technical grade — navy-base low saturation, cyan schematic accent on diagrams only, accurate workwear color, minimal stylistic grading, systems trust, no Observable clinical cast, no DAVID warm amber. Presenter addresses camera on warehouse bench, surprising failure mode or daily-use system hook. Camera: locked medium, direct address. Industrial window 4800K directional key locked — dust motes optional, deep shadow pockets, no magenta brick bounce, no cool fill contamination, zero hue drift. Industrial 4800K window key locked — deep shadow pockets for dimension, no magenta brick bounce, no cool fill contamination. COLOR LOCK Warehouse: brick #8B4A3B desaturated, concrete #A8A8A8, metal #5C5C5C, window light warm-neutral 4800K — no purple shadows, no teal concrete, no neon practicals. GRADE LOCK Blueprint-Technical: host skin true-to-life, wardrobe earth-neutral, diagram accents cyan #3DB8E8 on navy #0A1628 only — forbid full-frame warm lamp cast, forbid magenta fog, forbid orange global, forbid Observable grey clinical on host. Locked-off or slow linear track; no handheld. Lip-synced, delivers: "Your heat pump heats and cools from one outdoor box. The part that flips the whole cycle lives inside this valve — and most homeowners have never seen it." deep measured baritone, unhurried authoritative systems walk-through, synthetic presenter only. Finish on presentational gesture peak, never hold dead stillness or dead frame.
shots.speech_text: Your heat pump heats and cools from one outdoor box. The part that flips the whole cycle lives inside this valve — and most homeowners have never seen it.

Locked-off or slow linear track; no handheld. Lip-synced, delivers: "A four-way valve routes hot refrigerant gas either to the indoor coil for heat, or to the outdoor coil for cooling. A slide piston inside the body swaps those paths in milliseconds." deep measured baritone, unhurried authoritative systems walk-through, synthetic presenter only. shots.speech_text: A four-way valve routes hot refrigerant gas either to the indoor coil for heat, or to the outdoor coil for cooling. A slide piston inside the body swaps those paths in milliseconds.

shots.speech_text: ASHRAE documents the standard vapor-compression cycle. Manufacturer teardowns show the slide-and-capillary pilot that moves the piston when the thermostat calls for mode change.

shots.speech_text: Watch the payoff: refrigerant enters here, the slide shifts, and the flow diagram inverts — same compressor, opposite job. One valve, two seasons.
shots.diagram_prompt: Exploded four-way heat pump reversing valve: brass body, slide piston, solenoid pilot, capillary lines to coils; labeled flow arrows for heating vs cooling modes; cyan-on-navy schematic

shots.speech_text: When this valve sticks, you get heat in summer or ice in winter — and a service call that costs more than the part. Knowing the mechanism is knowing what failed.
